title: salvia erythrostoma subsp. isabelina
intro: Salvia erythrostoma subsp. isabelina, also known as Isabel's red salvia, is a plant species within the sage genus, native to Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Isabel's red salvia has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, offering benefits like reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced antioxidant defenses. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for easy incorporation into a holistic health regimen.
